steps.title: Set up the product
steps.content: Let the smaller integer be $x$. The larger integer is $2x+11$, so their product is $$x(2x+11)=546.$$

steps.title: Factor the quadratic
steps.content: Expand and move everything to one side: $$2x^2+11x-546=0.$$ Since $2\cdot 546=1092$ and $39\cdot 28=1092$, split the middle term: $$2x^2+39x-28x-546=0.$$

steps.title: Solve for the positive integer
steps.content: Factor by grouping: $$x(2x+39)-14(2x+39)=0$$ $$(x-14)(2x+39)=0.$$ Thus $x=14$ or $x=-\frac{39}{2}$. The smaller positive integer is $14$.

steps.title: Check the product
steps.content: The larger integer is $2(14)+11=39$, and $14\cdot39=546$. That matches choice B.
